source_jobs.title: Director of Sales and Business Development (Corporate Affairs + Politics)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE JOB



Palette is a fast-growing startup at the center of the creator economy. Our team of 80+ is working towards defining the standard for talent management and marketing in an era of new media dominated by digital-first entertainers.



Our management infrastructure mirrors the depth of attention given to the world’s biggest cultural and entertainment icons, and previously reserved for artists in traditional arenas of entertainment. Today, it services a roster of 200+ creators and influencers with a combined following of 1B+ serving over 45.8B impressions monthly. Our creators have starred in Super Bowl commercials, launched national brands, and have been featured in nearly every major publication.



Our marketing agency executes strategy for some of the most influential brands and organizations in the world, including: The Biden White House, Amazon, simplehuman, and the Gates Foundation. 42% of Americans under the age of 40 are no longer accessible through traditional marketing channels – we enable organizations to speak to those audiences. Our marketing team has been recognized with 5 Shorty Impact Awards, and has advised three US Presidents on the future of the internet.




THE ROLE



As the Director of Sales and Business Development, you will be responsible for driving marketing side revenue growth, building the sales pipeline from the ground up, and establishing a repeatable sales process. Over the past five years, Palette has become a leader in the creator economy, building strong relationships with thousands of brands and partners — all without a formal sales team. The person in this role will be responsible for building the structure, process, and strategy to turn inbound interest and targeted outbound outreach into scalable revenue.




RESPONSIBILITIES



 - Build and refine a sales playbook tailored to Palette’s marketing services, including prospecting approaches, outreach scripts, pricing tactics, and negotiation guidelines

 - Deliver on annual sales bookings and revenue targets by identifying and securing new client opportunities.

 - Create tailored proposals that address client challenges and position Palette as a strategic thought‑partner.

 - Negotiate campaign rates and develop scalable pricing structures that align with industry standards.

 - Prepare monthly/quarterly revenue forecasts and pipeline reports.

 - Monitor KPIs such as revenue generated, number and quality of leads, lead conversion rates, customer‑acquisition cost, and client retention.

 - Maintain strong relationships with C‑suite executives and key decision‑makers at client organization to ensure Palette is top of mind.

 - Serve as clients’ primary point of contact during the sales process and ensure a smooth handover to account managers or campaign managers.

 - Work closely cross-functionally to develop integrated campaigns and ensure that influencer strategies align with client goals.

 - As the business scales, hire and coach sales reps, setting clear performance expectations and providing ongoing training.




QUALIFICATIONS



 - 5–8+ years of experience in brand partnerships, media sales, new business development or talent representation—preferably within digital media, influencer marketing, or creator economy.

 - Bachelor's Degree in Marketing, Digital Marketing, Communications, Media Studies, or other closely related field

 - Proven track record of closing deals and managing high-level client relationships across brands, agencies, and media holding companies.

 - Strong understanding of the campaign planning cycle and RFP processes.

 - Deep knowledge of the influencer marketing landscape, with the ability to match the right talent to brand needs.

 - Confident negotiator with experience setting rates and managing deal structures at scale.

 - Ability to identify broader business needs, assess the revenue opportunity of deals, and project manage the full cycle of deals.

 - Highly organized and process-oriented,

source_jobs.title: Senior Director of Distribution
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE JOB



Palette is a fast-growing startup at the center of the creator economy. Our team of 80+ is working towards defining the standard for talent management and marketing in an era of new media dominated by digital-first entertainers.



Our management infrastructure mirrors the depth of attention given to the world’s biggest cultural and entertainment icons, and previously reserved for artists in traditional arenas of entertainment. Today, it services a roster of 200+ creators and influencers with a combined following of 1B+ serving over 45.8B impressions monthly. Our creators have starred in Super Bowl commercials, launched national brands, and have been featured in nearly every major publication.



Our marketing agency executes strategy for some of the most influential brands and organizations in the world, including: The Biden White House, Amazon, simplehuman, and the Gates Foundation. 42% of Americans under the age of 40 are no longer accessible through traditional marketing channels – we enable organizations to speak to those audiences. Our marketing team has been recognized with 5 Shorty Impact Awards, and has advised three US Presidents on the future of the internet.




THE ROLE



Palette Media is seeking a Senior Director of Distribution to lead how brands break through in an increasingly fragmented social landscape. This role sits at the center of creator marketing, content creation, and paid amplification—owning the strategy and execution behind how campaigns are distributed, scaled, and optimized for performance. You’ll be responsible for building and evolving innovative distribution frameworks that maximize reach, efficiency, and measurable impact across platforms.



You’ll lead end-to-end creator activations and oversee the full content distribution ecosystem—from UGC pipelines and paid media to emerging channels like clipping networks and meme ecosystems. We’re looking for a strategic operator and team leader who thrives in fast-moving environments, can translate client goals into high-performing campaigns, and consistently pushes the boundaries of how creator content is activated and amplified.



Responsibilities

 - Develop Distribution Strategies: Craft social media amplification strategies on behalf of our clients that meet their audience and performance goals and maximize their budget across channels, optimizing for cost-efficiency, performance, and measurable outcomes.

 - Vendor Management: Lead vendor and partner negotiations across creators, platforms, and paid media channels—driving favorable economics, scalable pricing structures, and long-term partnerships

 - Creator Marketing Activations: Own end-to-end development, execution, and optimization of creator-driven campaigns—proactively driving timelines, identifying next steps, and ensuring campaigns deliver against performance goals without requiring direction.

 - Content & Distribution Strategy: Oversee UGC content pipelines, paid media amplification, clipping and amplification accounts, and other innovative distribution methods to ensure visibility and impact. Own and optimize campaign performance from a distribution perspective across all channels and tactics. Identify, test, and scale emerging distribution tactics (e.g., clipping networks, meme ecosystems, new platform behaviors).

 - Team Leadership: Lead and mentor a high-performing team responsible for sourcing creators, managing activations, and sustaining industry relationships. Set the pace, drive accountability, and proactively remove blockers—modeling ownership and follow-through in all workstreams. Develop and refine new processes as needed.

 - Industry Relationships: Build and maintain strong partnerships with creators, independent talent, management companies, agencies, and other partners to stay at the forefront of creator culture.  

 - Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work cross-functionally with business development, client services, strategy, and data to align on

source_jobs.title: Director of Client Services (Corporate Affairs + Politics)
source_jobs.description:
About the Job



Palette is a fast-growing startup at the center of the creator economy. Our team of 80+ is working towards defining the standard for talent management and marketing in an era of new media dominated by digital-first entertainers.



Our management infrastructure mirrors the depth of attention given to the world’s biggest cultural and entertainment icons, and previously reserved for artists in traditional arenas of entertainment. Today, it services a roster of 200+ creators and influencers with a combined following of 1B+ serving over 45.8B impressions monthly. Our creators have starred in Super Bowl commercials, launched national brands, and have been featured in nearly every major publication.



Our marketing agency executes strategy for some of the most influential brands and organizations in the world, including: The Biden White House, Amazon, simplehuman, and the Gates Foundation. 42% of Americans under the age of 40 are no longer accessible through traditional marketing channels – we enable organizations to speak to those audiences. Our marketing team has been recognized with 5 Shorty Impact Awards, and has advised three US Presidents on the future of the internet.



About the Role



Palette is seeking a Director of Client Services to lead strategic partnerships across corporate affairs, public affairs, and political communications. This senior leadership role sits at the intersection of creator marketing, digital culture, and high-stakes communications, helping influential brands and organizations reach audiences through emerging media.



You will serve as a trusted advisor to enterprise-level clients, leading integrated campaigns from strategy through execution while managing complex stakeholder relationships and cross-functional teams. The ideal candidate is both a strong operator and strategic thinker, with experience navigating fast-paced environments, overseeing high-impact communications initiatives, and driving long-term client growth.



Responsibilities



Client Leadership + Strategic Advisory

 - Account Management: As the main point of contact for clients, you are responsible for the overall success and strategic management of client accounts.

 - Strategy: Serve as a senior strategic advisor to clients, building trusted relationships with executive stakeholders and guiding long-term partnership growth. Lead high-stakes client communications, issue management, and strategic decision-making.

 - Performance: Oversee campaign performance analysis and provide strategic recommendations based on data, reporting, and audience insights. Ensure campaigns deliver measurable impact aligned with client KPIs and business objectives.

 - Client Deliverables: Oversee the development of client deliverables, including memos, presentations, creator briefs, and campaign messaging, while providing guidance and editorial oversight to junior team members.

 - Business Operations: Oversee client budgets, contracting, and invoicing to ensure operational accuracy and alignment with client scope and budget parameters.



Cross-Functional Leadership

 - Cross-Department Coordination: Partner cross-functionally with teams across strategy, business development, talent management, production, distribution, and measurement to deliver integrated campaigns.

 - Processes: Improve internal systems, workflows, and operational processes to support scalable growth.

 - Internal Leadership: As a member of senior leadership, help shape team culture, operational processes, and the continued growth of Palette.



Business Development + Industry Leadership

 - New Business: Support agency growth through participation in pitches, proposals, and strategic business development initiatives.

 - Upsell Opportunities: Identify opportunities to expand existing client relationships and drive long-term account growth.

 - External Events: Represent Palette externally through thought leadership, speaking engagements, industry events, and r

source_jobs.title: Revenue Operations Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE JOB



Palette is a fast-growing startup at the center of the creator economy. Our team of 80+ is working towards defining the standard for talent management and marketing in an era of new media dominated by digital-first entertainers.



Our management infrastructure mirrors the depth of attention given to the world’s biggest cultural and entertainment icons, and previously reserved for artists in traditional arenas of entertainment. Today, it services a roster of 200+ creators and influencers with a combined following of 1B+ serving over 45.8B impressions monthly. Our creators have starred in Super Bowl commercials, launched national brands, and have been featured in nearly every major publication.



Our marketing agency executes strategy for some of the most influential brands and organizations in the world, including: The Biden White House, Amazon, simplehuman, and the Gates Foundation. 42% of Americans under the age of 40 are no longer accessible through traditional marketing channels – we enable organizations to speak to those audiences. Our marketing team has been recognized with 5 Shorty Impact Awards, and has advised three US Presidents on the future of the internet.




ABOUT THE ROLE



We’re hiring our first Revenue Operations leader—a highly strategic and hands-on operator who will build and scale our RevOps function from the ground up. This is a department-of-one role (for now) with significant ownership and visibility across the organization. You’ll partner closely with Sales, Talent, Partnerships, Finance, and Leadership to bring structure, clarity, and scalability to how we generate and manage revenue.



You’ll own HubSpot, define our revenue data model, improve pipeline visibility, and create the operational backbone that enables smarter, faster decision-making. This role is ideal for someone who thrives in 0→1 environments, enjoys building systems from scratch, and has experience navigating the nuances of the entertainment / creator ecosystem.




RESPONSIBILITIES



Revenue Systems & Infrastructure

 - Own and optimize HubSpot (CRM architecture, workflows, automation, integrations)

 - Design and implement scalable processes across the full revenue lifecycle (lead → deal → revenue)

 - Evaluate and manage additional tools across the GTM stack (e.g., attribution, forecasting, reporting)



Data & Analytics

 - Define and maintain a clean, reliable source of truth for revenue data

 - Build dashboards and reporting to track pipeline health, conversion rates, deal velocity, and revenue performance

 - Translate data into actionable insights for leadership and GTM teams



Pipeline & Forecasting

 - Establish forecasting methodologies and pipeline management best practices

 - Identify gaps and inefficiencies in funnel performance and drive improvements

 - Partner with leadership on revenue planning and goal-setting



Cross-Functional Alignment

 - Act as the connective tissue between Sales, Talent, Partnerships, and Finance

 - Standardize processes and definitions across teams (e.g., stages, KPIs, attribution)

 - Support compensation planning, territory design, and performance tracking



Build & Scale RevOps

 - Create the RevOps roadmap and prioritize initiatives based on business impact

 - Lay the foundation for future team growth (hiring, tooling, processes)

 - Bring structure to a fast-moving, sometimes ambiguous environment




QUALIFICATIONS



Core Experience

 - 5–10+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Operations, or related roles

 - Bachelor's Degree in Business Administration, Data Analytics, Marketing, Finance, Economics or closely related field

 - Experience as a foundational or early RevOps hire (or operating with high autonomy)

 - Deep hands-on experience with HubSpot

 - Strong data fluency—comfortable working with datasets, building reports, and identifying trends

 - Experience designing dashboards and forecasting models

 - Familiarity with integrations and data flows (w

source_jobs.title: Accounts Payable Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE JOB



Palette is a fast-growing startup at the center of the creator economy. Our team of 80+ is working towards defining the standard for talent management and marketing in an era of new media dominated by digital-first entertainers.



Our management infrastructure mirrors the depth of attention given to the world’s biggest cultural and entertainment icons, and previously reserved for artists in traditional arenas of entertainment. Today, it services a roster of 200+ creators and influencers with a combined following of 1B+ serving over 45.8B impressions monthly. Our creators have starred in Super Bowl commercials, launched national brands, and have been featured in nearly every major publication.



Our marketing agency executes strategy for some of the most influential brands and organizations in the world, including: The Biden White House, Amazon, simplehuman, and the Gates Foundation. 42% of Americans under the age of 40 are no longer accessible through traditional marketing channels – we enable organizations to speak to those audiences. Our marketing team has been recognized with 5 Shorty Impact Awards, and has advised three US Presidents on the future of the internet.




THE ROLE



As an Accounts Payable Manager, you will own and scale the accounts payable function for a fast-growing creator economy business. This is a highly autonomous role responsible for managing the complete AP lifecycle while building the processes, controls, and documentation needed to support a rapidly growing organization.



You will serve as the primary owner of vendor payments, invoice processing, reconciliations, and AP-related reporting, while partnering closely with Finance, Operations, Talent, and department leaders across the business. Beyond day-to-day execution, you'll help create structure, improve workflows, and implement scalable systems that increase efficiency and strengthen financial controls.



This role is ideal for someone who enjoys building order from complexity, takes pride in operational excellence, and wants to have a meaningful impact on how finance infrastructure evolves as the company grows.




RESPONSIBILITIES




ACCOUNTS PAYABLE OPERATIONS



 - Own the full-cycle accounts payable process, including invoice intake, coding, approvals, payment processing, vendor maintenance, and reconciliations.

 - Ensure invoices are processed accurately and timely in accordance with company policies and approval workflows.

 - Manage vendor payments via ACH, wire transfers, checks, and other approved payment methods.

 - Monitor AP aging and proactively manage upcoming payment obligations, payment terms, and vendor communications.

 - Reconcile vendor statements, investigate discrepancies, and resolve issues with both vendors and internal stakeholders.

 - Maintain accurate vendor records, including W-9s, payment details, contracts, tax documentation, and supporting files.

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for AP-related inquiries from vendors, employees, and finance leadership.




MONTH-END CLOSE & FINANCIAL SUPPORT



 - Support month-end and year-end close activities through AP accruals, reconciliations, aging reviews, and expense reporting.

 - Prepare schedules and supporting documentation required for financial reporting and close processes.

 - Assist with 1099 preparation and other compliance-related reporting requirements.

 - Support audits by preparing AP schedules, reconciliations, and requested documentation.

 - Maintain accurate records that improve close readiness and reporting reliability.




PROCESS IMPROVEMENT & SYSTEMS



 - Identify opportunities to improve AP workflows through automation, system enhancements, and process optimization.

 - Create, maintain, and improve AP documentation, including SOPs, process maps, approval workflows, payment procedures, close checklists, and escalation paths.

 - Build scalable processes that reduce manual effort while improving accuracy and visibili

notes: No tech roles posted, high volume of sales and business development roles
